meaning: adj.生而自由的,自由民的,适于自由民的
examples: Only freeborn male citizens could take part in the Olympic Games.
Until the last of my days, my proudest boast will ever be that I was the wife of Freeborn John Lilburne.
There is this real fear that large standing armies could ultimately compromise the rights of freeborn Englishmen.
Freeborn's death was confirmed by Nick Maley, a family friend who worked as his assistant in the 1970s.
Freeborn's son Graham became a make-up artist in his own right, working alongside his father on the Star Wars and Superman films.
